findings.description: Storage account allows anonymous public read access. Anyone on the internet can read sensitive files without authentication.
findings.remediation: Disable public blob access at the storage account level.
portalSteps: Set 'Allow Blob public access' to Disabled
cliCommands: az storage account update --name prod-storage-01 --resource-group rg-prod --allow-blob-public-access false

findings.description: HTTP connections are allowed. Data transferred over HTTP is exposed to man-in-the-middle attacks.
findings.remediation: Enable 'Secure transfer required' to force HTTPS only.
cliCommands: az storage account update --name staging-storage-01 --resource-group rg-staging --https-only true

findings.description: Windows Server 2019 has 14 critical patches pending for 108+ days. Unpatched systems are actively exploited.
findings.remediation: Apply all critical and security patches immediately using Azure Update Manager.
portalSteps: Select all Critical patches and schedule update
cliCommands: az vm assess-patches --name vm-web-01 --resource-group rg-prod
az vm install-patches --name vm-web-01 --resource-group rg-prod --maximum-duration PT2H --classifications-to-include-win Critical Security
validationSteps: Re-run assess-patches and confirm 0 critical patches pending

findings.description: Port 22 (SSH) is open to 0.0.0.0/0. This exposes all VMs to brute-force attacks from the entire internet.
findings.remediation: Restrict SSH access to specific trusted IP ranges only. Consider using Azure Bastion instead.
portalSteps: Change Source from Any to your VPN CIDR
cliCommands: az network nsg rule delete --resource-group rg-prod --nsg-name nsg-web --name Allow-SSH-Any

az network nsg rule create --resource-group rg-prod --nsg-name nsg-web --name Allow-SSH-VPN --priority 200 --source-address-prefixes 10.0.0.0/8 --destination-port-ranges 22 --access Allow --protocol Tcp
validationSteps: Verify no rule allows port 22 from 0.0.0.0/0
Test SSH from outside allowed range fails

findings.description: SQL firewall rule allows 0.0.0.0-255.255.255.255. Database is reachable from any IP on the internet.
findings.remediation: Delete AllowAllIPs firewall rule and add specific IP ranges only.
portalSteps: Add specific IP rules for your app servers
cliCommands: az sql server firewall-rule delete --server sql-dev-exposed --resource-group rg-dev --name AllowAllIps
az sql server firewall-rule create --server sql-dev-exposed --resource-group rg-dev --name Allow-App --start-ip-address 10.1.0.4 --end-ip-address 10.1.0.4
validationSteps: Test connection from unlisted IP fails

findings.description: Service principal has Owner role at subscription scope. Compromised credentials grant full control over all resources.
findings.remediation: Downscope to Contributor role limited to rg-prod only.
portalSteps: Find sp-prod-deployer Owner role and delete it
Re-assign as Contributor scoped to rg-prod only
cliCommands: az role assignment delete --assignee sp-prod-deployer --role Owner --scope /subscriptions/sub-123
az role assignment create --assignee sp-prod-deployer --role Contributor --scope /subscriptions/sub-123/resourceGroups/rg-prod
validationSteps: Test SP can still deploy to rg-prod

findings.description: Port 3389 (RDP) is open to 0.0.0.0/0. RDP exposed to internet is a top attack vector for ransomware.
findings.remediation: Delete or restrict RDP rule to specific trusted IPs. Use Azure Bastion instead.
cliCommands: az network nsg rule delete --resource-group rg-prod --nsg-name nsg-app --name Allow-RDP-Any
validationSteps: Verify no wildcard RDP rule exists
Test RDP from external IP fails

findings.description: Storage account lacks lifecycle policy for data retention and cleanup.
findings.remediation: Configure lifecycle management policy for automatic deletion.
portalSteps: Create rule for auto-deletion or archival
cliCommands: az storage account management-policy create --account-name prod-storage-02 --resource-group rg-prod --policy @policy.json

findings.description: Multiple inbound rules allow overly broad source ranges.
findings.remediation: Review and restrict rules to least privilege principle.
portalSteps: Restrict source IPs to only what's needed
cliCommands: az network nsg rule list --resource-group rg-staging --nsg-name nsg-staging

findings.description: Network flow logs are disabled. Cannot audit traffic or troubleshoot connectivity issues.
findings.remediation: Enable NSG flow logs and send to Log Analytics.
cliCommands: az network watcher flow-log create --resource-group rg-staging --enabled true --nsg nsg-staging --storage-account <storage-id>
validationSteps: Verify flow logs are enabled and logging

findings.description: Cosmos DB is accessible from all networks. Public endpoint is unrestricted.
findings.remediation: Enable firewall and restrict access to specific networks or IPs.
cliCommands: az cosmosdb update --name cosmos-staging --resource-group rg-staging --ip-range-filter 10.0.0.0/8
validationSteps: Verify access from unauthorized IP is blocked

findings.description: Public access is enabled without restrictions on cosmos-staging database.
findings.remediation: Disable public access or restrict to VNet.
portalSteps: Go to Cosmos DB Networking settings
cliCommands: az cosmosdb update --name cosmos-staging --resource-group rg-staging --enable-public-endpoint false

findings.description: Purge protection is disabled. Secrets can be permanently deleted before retention expires.
findings.remediation: Enable purge protection immediately.
cliCommands: az keyvault update --name kv-prod-secrets --resource-group rg-prod --enable-purge-protection true

findings.description: Key Vault is accessible from anywhere. Network access is unrestricted.
findings.remediation: Enable firewall and add VNet/IP rules.
portalSteps: Enable firewall and add VNet rules
cliCommands: az keyvault network-rule add --resource-group rg-prod --name kv-prod-secrets --vnet-name myVNet --subnet mySubnet

findings.description: OS disk is not encrypted. Data at rest is vulnerable.
findings.remediation: Enable Azure Disk Encryption using Key Vault.
cliCommands: az vm encryption enable --name vm-app-01 --resource-group rg-prod --disk-encryption-keyvault kv-prod-secrets

findings.description: OS disk is not encrypted on staging VM.
findings.remediation: Enable Azure Disk Encryption.
cliCommands: az vm encryption enable --name vm-staging-01 --resource-group rg-staging --disk-encryption-keyvault kv-prod-secrets

findings.description: DDoS Protection Standard is not enabled on vnet-dev-01.
findings.remediation: Enable DDoS Protection Standard (additional cost).
cliCommands: az network ddos-protection create --resource-group rg-dev --name ddos-protection

findings.description: Network flow logs not enabled on vnet-dev-01.
findings.remediation: Enable flow logs for network auditing.
cliCommands: az network watcher flow-log create --resource-group rg-dev --enabled true --target-resource-id <vnet-id>

findings.description: SQL server does not enforce Azure AD authentication.
findings.remediation: Enable Azure AD authentication and disable SQL auth.
portalSteps: Set AAD admin in Azure Active Directory admin settings
cliCommands: az sql server ad-admin create --resource-group rg-prod --server-name sql-prod --display-name 'AAD Admin' --object-id <object-id>

findings.description: TLS version should be upgraded to 1.3.
findings.remediation: Set minimal TLS version to 1.3.
cliCommands: az sql server update --resource-group rg-prod --name sql-prod --minimal-tls-version 1.3

findings.description: Storage account should have backup policy configured.
findings.remediation: Ensure backups are configured.
portalSteps: Configure backup policy in Storage Account
cliCommands: # Configure backup policy

findings.description: Review additional NSG rules for compliance.
findings.remediation: Audit all rules for least privilege.
portalSteps: Review all NSG rules in nsg-web
cliCommands: az network nsg rule list --resource-group rg-prod --nsg-name nsg-web

findings.description: Review Log Analytics retention policy to ensure it meets compliance requirements.
findings.remediation: Adjust retention days based on compliance needs.
portalSteps: Go to Usage and estimated costs
cliCommands: az monitor log-analytics workspace update --resource-group rg-prod --workspace-name log-analytics-prod --retention-time 365

findings.description: Verify antimalware is configured and enabled.
findings.remediation: Install and configure Microsoft Defender for Cloud endpoint protection.
cliCommands: az vm extension list --resource-group rg-prod --vm-name vm-app-01

findings.description: Blob versioning is disabled. Cannot recover previous versions of files.
findings.remediation: Enable blob versioning for data protection.
cliCommands: az storage blob service-properties update --account-name prod-storage-02 --enable-versioning
